transcript.text: I'm very productive, so I'm excited to come out of this and immediately turn to Microsoft the next week.
But actually, I ran into Marcel and Daniel on the street, so that was very fun.
I walked with one my team members and was like, Marcel!
Oh, this is amazing.
Where is this on the off-site in San Francisco?
I mean, yeah, so it's on-site at our office.
Okay, cool.
well, I will jump right into it.
Let's do it.
Yeah, just running through the updates.
We have eight articles held for this week.
We started working on the technical audit.
This is not on your website.
It's actually the pre-audit so we can validate what all we do.
Yeah.
So, this actually, well, this agenda was created for Tuesday, so we're actually done with this.
But, yeah.
Great.
And then the new topics that were proposed, I responded to your comments on AirCable.
Please take a look at it, where you have a chance.
And then the new topics around MRM and model validation for the financial services cluster, we'll have that up by Tuesday next week.
I may not have a chance to review topics until early next week or mid-next week anyways, so no rush in there.
I feel like you guys have plenty of stuff to work on in a couple weeks at least, and I can make sure I catch up on whatever ongoing topics we have.
Yeah, no problem.
Yeah.
So running for the performance, we had another great week, all-time highs in views and sessions.
So it's generally increasing quite well.
The GSC data also is kind of showing sort of a rise.
So it's nice to have some agreement between both these platforms, so just if we're going in the right place.
LLM referral traffic also hit a new high, 226 sessions.
And generally, we're noticing it's the same top-performing blogs and SEO that are doing well in the LLMs as well.
Something we're noticing with some of the other clients is that higher LLM traffic is not necessarily leading to more conversions in terms of downloads or whatever.
Seems to be more of a brand awareness thing.
We think, it's still too early to say, but we think one of the reasons for this might just be that people at LLMs are looking for, like, how do I do this?
Or, like, very, yeah, definition-based things.
It kind of falls up with what people are saying broadly as well across the SEO world.
So we're just kind of looking at it.
I do have some ideas on how to maybe drill deeper into this and see the impact of it, but it's still WIP.
So I'll have something up, you know, maybe next week or within the next couple of weeks.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Generally, though, content engagement is super high.
For this past week, for example, I mean, these numbers are pretty massive.
Like, it's like, you know, 900 sessions on Google and 82% of them were engaged.
It's pretty, like, yeah, we don't see these numbers.
So usually I'm used to seeing something closer to like 50%, 60%, but this is like 80, 90%.
So pretty good stuff over here.
And then in terms of new keywords that we're ranking for, you know, character error rate, birth score, and agentic AI, now we're in the top five for these particular keyword searches.
So that's pretty good news.
And of course, this is over and above where we're showing up in the LLMs as well as AI reviews.
So generally things are good.
Any questions I can answer about all of this?
No, I think you guys are doing a great job.
I know we're gonna have a bit of a backlog of content review on our end.
And, um, Thank you,
We're kind of focused on the cast right now.

I'll share with you my worry, which is that we're going to massively screw up the site's SEO rankings through our migration here based off of, A, how our sanity instance has been set up and the fact that it's, that they just vibricated.
We've had to, like, we're doing, I have to do, like, lot of manual QA, and I think that's where I'm hoping we'll identify some stuff, and we're making some fixes.

And while I think the brand's going to be massively up-leveled and the long-term, this will be positive long-term, I am worried that we're going to mess up your good work here for a couple weeks while we get everything figured out.
No worries.
We'll recover it back, so.

Right now, I'm just going through a, I've kind of QA'd and prioritized all of our top blogs based off of traffic in the last week through, on our side, and we're starting to work our way down for everything else that's getting clicked.
Other than that, we're making, we're going to make sure that all of the, like, SEO...
So metadata and SEO titles get ported over accurately, and then it's going to be, I think, a redirect squashing game and some other stuff that we'll have to deal with once things get over.
But my expectation, our drop dead for the website is going to be Monday, just getting it over, and then it's, I think, going to be a week or two of us trying to get it in a shape.
Okay.
Yeah, generally, I think we're in good shape to start the audit and then start changing the assets as well in the blocks, just images and CTAs and all that.
Something I wanted to check with we are recreating a lot of the images, too.
So like, you'll see, I think I shared with you the new mastering agency image, a couple other things.
we'll have updated ones for things like the leaderboard, cetera, that are coming.
Okay, I just wanted to quickly check, I know we spoke about this, but the podcast, should we, the podcast blog, should we just completely stop that now?
Or do you just want to do that?
We're not really seeing traffic from it.
And I think we're not driving people there anyways.
When we're doing podcasts, we want them to actually go consume it, typically a video of it.
So I guess we could be using...
Oh, okay.
The only reason I would think about continuing it would be to directly capture the traffic versus sending it to YouTube because we can embed the YouTube videos on the blogs.
And so we could be doing that.
But the problem is that our turnaround times on it isn't...
Not because of you, but just broadly because of how the process works.
It's not like the blog was getting up the same morning as a podcast.
And so most of the promo that happens in the first week anyways.
So I feel like there's not enough value of trying to force ourselves to capture what minimal referral traffic that we're getting from LinkedIn anyways.
And so it's like, okay, let's just reprioritize that to always on.
Versus saying, we're going to keep trying to do this social thing.
doesn't quite work from a timing perspective.
We can always pick it back up at some point if we're like, hey, we really want to capture this.
But I'm fine with folks showing up to our YouTube and helping subscribe there and feeding their way back in as far as our social referrals for the pod.
And instead, let's focus on more long-tail stuff, since I think that's going to have probably more value for us anyways.

Yeah, and I think in terms of the podcast as well, the right way to do it, at least if you ask me, is incorporating those quotes into our existing blogs and then use it to actually enrich our SEO stuff.
Yeah, we can look at that.
think trying to incorporate more of the podcast video and quotes throughout our existing blog system is a good call.
And that's something I think we can look at in early June.
We'll have a campaign team member who's coming in June 2nd.
And one of the first things I'm going to have him do is come in.
And start working with you more closely.
I'm like, okay, how do we enrich all of this content to keep it going deeper, keep it more tightly aligned to our funnel?
And doing some of the stuff like, you know, for example, like I mentioned that for Observe and Evaluate modules are going away and we're just calling in our platform.
So like, how do we want to start updating the product?
So definitely, I think a lot of opportunities on that.
I think it's fine for the next couple weeks here to say, okay, like, we're going to the website launch.
How do we netting QA and fix what we need to on the technical side and or what we may have messed up on the blog side?
And then what do we need to do from there?
I think we'll have a big backlog of content and kind of get live.
And then Jack will come in and we'll start working on like, okay, like, spend a lot of June on how do we make this funnel tighter and how do we continue with it?
Okay.
Yeah, definitely.
There's something we can do to, like, make that process easier.
Definitely let us know.
We've already created, like, a list of top-performing blogs and we have.
The podcast transcripts, anyway, we're kind of running it through our flows just to find the right insertion points, so we should have that up next week.

Yeah, I'm wondering if part of what we should do, and this is not a short-term thing, this is, again, two weeks out, but do we look at what are our top 10 podcast videos and top five how-to videos, and then, hey, where are they matching?
For example, Aaron on my team put out a monitor your LangChain agent with Galileo tutorial, could we be embedding that in any blog that mentions LangChain type of thing?

So I think we can go through, similarly in June, look at a list of all those blogs, or all those videos, and say, hey, which one of these perform, which of these align to a topic that maybe is part of a cluster, or in a couple of blogs, and then they go through an embed.
Yeah, definitely, can do that for sure, and we've actually scraped your YouTube videos, like all of them, so, for example, the example you took, which is mentioning the LangChain.
I think we can, I think, reasonably well identify such blocks because we just kind of, that'll be an automatic insertion in our flow.
So we can say like, hey, if this is happening, YouTube too.
And we have, we have like webinars and stuff too.
So it's like, I know a lot of those are not necessarily going to align, but like some of them, I think will start to be good fits.
So yeah, happy to leverage those wherever we can.
think that's a good, that's a good flow process to that.
Yeah.
Well, that was pretty much the update.
Yeah.
For next week, we're going to prioritize the reviews one when the migration is done.
yeah.
Yeah.
I'll get you guys access Monday or Tuesday.
So we can start getting through stuff more extensively, probably by Monday.
Who all, as far as like who to add, team at growthx labs is fine or do you need a couple people outside?
Team at, team at growthx is fine.
Cool.
Great.
Awesome.
Thanks so much Vivek.
And, uh, we have a great yesterday.
All right.
Cool.
Enjoy the office.
Have happy weekend.
Thanks.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Galileo and GrowthX to discuss content performance, website migration, and strategy updates.](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content performance hit all-time highs in views and sessions, with exceptionally high engagement rates (80-90%)](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=97.0)
  - [Galileo's website migration may temporarily impact SEO rankings; QA and fixes are ongoing](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=242.0)
  - [Podcast blog strategy shifting to focus on incorporating content into existing blogs and enriching SEO](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=350.0)
  - [Plans to integrate top-performing video content into relevant blog posts in early June](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=548.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance Update

  - [All-time highs in views and sessions](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=97.0)
  - [GSC data showing positive trends, aligning with other platform metrics](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=108.0)
  - [LLM referral traffic reached new high of 226 sessions](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Exceptionally high content engagement: 900 sessions on Google with 82% engaged](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=189.0)
  - [New top 5 keyword rankings: character error rate, birth score, agentic AI](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=210.0)
  - [LLM traffic not necessarily leading to more conversions; potentially more brand awareness-focused](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)

### Website Migration Concerns

  - [Conor expressed worry about potential SEO impact due to Sanity instance setup](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=242.0)
  - [Manual QA process underway to identify and fix issues](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
  - [Drop-dead date for website migration: Monday](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=311.0)
  - [1-2 weeks expected for post-migration adjustments and fixes](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=311.0)
  - [Focus on accurate metadata, SEO titles, and redirect management](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)

### Content Strategy Updates

  - [Recreating and updating images for various content pieces (e.g., mastering agency, leaderboard)](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=334.0)
  - [Discontinuing separate podcast blog posts due to low traffic and timing issues](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=350.0)
  - [Shifting focus to incorporating podcast quotes and video content into existing blogs](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)
  - [Plans to enrich content and align it more closely with the sales funnel in early June](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)

### Video Content Integration

  - [Proposal to embed top-performing podcast and how-to videos in relevant blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=548.0)
  - [GrowthX has scraped all YouTube videos to facilitate automatic insertion in relevant blogs](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=587.0)
  - [Consideration of webinar content for potential integration](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to have 8 articles ready for the week](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=37.0)
  - [Complete technical pre-audit of Galileo's website](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=42.0)
  - [Finalize new topics on MRM and model validation for financial services cluster by Tuesday](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=69.0)
  - [Conor to provide GrowthX team access to new website by Monday/Tuesday](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=639.0)
  - [GrowthX to prioritize reviews content once migration is complete](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=634.0)
  - [Prepare for content enrichment and funnel alignment work with new campaign team member in early June](https://fathom.video/share/6s4pDgM_weQEBBKt2nU7Ro5EmY9RoQoR?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)

action_items.description: QA and prioritize top blogs based on last week's traffic for website migration
Ensure SEO metadata and titles are accurately ported over during website migration
Prepare list of top-performing blogs and podcast transcripts with insertion points for enrichment
Create list of top 10 podcast videos and top 5 how-to videos for potential blog embedding
Prioritize reviews content when website migration is complete
Provide GrowthX team access to new website by Monday/Tuesday for content review
